name: IKEA
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of IKEA's brand language — the Swedish flat-pack democracy. Canvas is pure white. The brand-asset trinity is the IKEA blue (#0058a3), the IKEA yellow (#ffdb00), and the wordmark lockup of the two used together at full saturation on the navigation. Type runs a custom geometric grotesk (Noto IKEA / Verdana) at a precise scale. Photography is room-staging — fully furnished rooms with real-looking models, kids, dogs, breakfast tables — never product-on-white. The voice is 'practical, democratic, Swedish' — a meatball-and-flatpack-instructions sensibility wrapped in clean editorial scale.

design_language.motion_signature: Carousel-driven motion. Room-photograph carousels auto-advance every 6 seconds with a slow 800ms cross-fade. Hover on product tiles raises a 4px soft shadow over 200ms. CTA buttons have a hover background-darken from #0058a3 to #003e72 over 150ms. Page transitions are flat fades — no kinetic scroll-fanfare.

trademark_signals: IKEA blue (#0058a3) + IKEA yellow (#ffdb00) lockup on the navigation — the most recognizable brand-color pair in retail, used at full saturation on the brand mark
Room-staging photography — never product-on-white; always a fully-furnished room with real-looking inhabitants — children eating cereal, parents reading, dogs sleeping
Pricing in a tag-shape with a yellow background — the iconic IKEA price tag — used on product cards and circulars
Inspirational-tag taxonomy — products grouped by 'lifestyle vibe' (e.g., 'Small spaces', 'Smart storage') rather than purely by function — the catalog reads as lifestyle advice
Dense product-grid catalog pages with low-cost shipping callouts in the yellow tag — the price is the brand voltage
ai_slop_avoided: Don't show product-on-white photography. IKEA photography is always in-context — a room, a kitchen, a kids' bedroom with kids in it
Don't make the IKEA yellow into a secondary accent. The yellow is reserved for price tags and the wordmark — using it as a section background is off-brand
Don't lighten the IKEA blue. The brand blue is locked at #0058a3 and is the only acceptable navigation-bar fill
Don't substitute Noto IKEA / Verdana with a fashion-editorial serif. The brand voice is geometric-grotesk practical, not magazine-editorial
Don't drop the price visibility. IKEA's brand voltage IS the low price; hiding it or rendering it small reads as off-brand
